message:
test(e2e): save → restart → restore roundtrip tests (wa-bo6f)

The existing snapshot_e2e suite exercises capture and load paths but
never crosses the actual restart boundary — the moment where the
SnapshotEngine and SessionRestorer handles die with the mux process
and a fresh process has to reconstitute session identity from the
SQLite file alone. The bead's core invariant ("ft snapshot save →
restart → ft snapshot restore survives scrollback, layout, and
session identity") was therefore unverified.

Add three new tests that each capture with engine A in a scope, drop
the engine (simulating mux exit), then build a fresh engine + restorer
pair against the same DB and assert survival of a specific fidelity
axis:

1. `e2e_save_restart_restore_session_identity_survives_engine_rebuild`
   - 3-pane topology across 2 windows
   - asserts session_id / checkpoint_id / pane_id set / cwd map all
     match byte-for-byte after the engine rebuild
   - runs the real SessionRestorer.restore against MockWezterm
   - confirms post-restore detect() returns None (shutdown_clean flip)

2. `e2e_save_restart_restore_scrollback_bytes_survive_engine_rebuild`
   - seeds output_segments with ANSI colors, tabs, UTF-8 emoji +
     accented characters to catch byte-level loss
   - asserts each segment appears in order in the replayed content and
     scrollback_restored_count / scrollback_error are clean across the
     boundary

3. `e2e_save_restart_restore_nested_split_topology_preserved`
   - drives inputs from the checked-in complex_layout fixture
   - asserts topology_json re-parses into an identical tree (self-
     roundtrip) and that layout_result.pane_id_map covers every leaf
     pane across the HSplit / VSplit nesting

Each test emits a structured E2ETestReport on [E2E_REPORT] lines with
per-phase timing so a CI log alone is enough to diagnose regressions
(per the real-service / no-mocks harness methodology).

## Shape

- No mocks for persistence: real tempfile SQLite, real schema.
- Mock only for the terminal surface (MockWezterm), the same pattern
  the rest of the suite already uses — there's no in-process wezterm
  available to integration tests.
- Zero new dependencies.
- Tests are self-contained in snapshot_e2e.rs and re-use the existing
  helpers (setup_test_db, make_pane, add_phase, hash_text,
  emit_report, collect_fixture_panes).

## Verification surface

Local compile verification was blocked by 69 pre-existing errors in
crates/frankenterm-core/src/runtime.rs (other in-flight agents:
missing tokio::sync::mpsc imports, StreamingTasks type incomplete —
drain / insert / remove / contains_key / keys methods not yet
ported). The test file itself adds only calls that mirror the
existing e2e_* tests (SnapshotEngine::new, engine.capture,
SessionRestorer::{detect,load_checkpoint,restore}, MockWezterm,
TopologySnapshot::{from_json,to_json}) — no new API surface is
touched. Shipping the tests now so they become live the moment
runtime.rs compiles again.

## Bead

wa-bo6f is formally blocked by wa-1igc (in_progress, FoggyRobin), so
this is landed as bounded support-lane work in the snapshot_e2e.rs
file only — same pattern MossyPine used for prior wa-bo6f additions
(2026-04-07 comment on the bead).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
